description_long: This object is a small, cylindrical medicine vial with a slightly tapered neck and a flat, metallic cap. The body of the vial is made of translucent or opaque white material, likely glass or plastic, and features a printed label with medical information, including a QR code and red and black text. The cap appears to be metallic, possibly aluminum, and is crimped securely around the neck, suggesting it is designed to be tamper-evident. The label is affixed to one side, with the rest of the vial surface left blank. The overall design is sterile and utilitarian, typical for injectable medicines or vaccines. The vial is compact and portable, suitable for use in healthcare settings or for distribution in urban environments.
description: A small, white cylindrical medicine vial with a metallic cap and a printed label, designed for sterile storage of injectable medicine.
description_view_0: Front view showing the printed label with text, QR code, and dosage information clearly visible.
description_view_1: Angled side view with the label partially visible, showing the curvature of the vial and the metallic cap.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain, unlabelled white surface and the full shape of the vial.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the edge of the label just visible, highlighting the smooth, blank surface and cap.
